name: Zero-Config Modeling Migration
description: Migrate the project from a repo-local, config-first DDD spec tool shape to a zero-config modeling product with a fixed user path.

userStories.title: Switch repository to zero-config directory convention
userStories.description: As a repository maintainer, I want the current repository itself to adopt the target product's fixed input and output conventions so that internal usage matches the future user experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Move the current repo-owned canonical source from design-spec/canonical/ to ddd-spec/canonical/.
Move the current repo-owned generated outputs from design-spec/artifacts/ and design-spec/generated/ to ./.ddd-spec/artifacts/ and ./.ddd-spec/generated/.
Ensure the primary repo workflow no longer requires editing design-spec/canonical/ or reading design-spec/artifacts/ as the user path.
Keep the example domains under examples/ as regression inputs; they do not need to become the user entry path in this story.
After the migration, the repository can still generate bundle, analysis, viewer, and TypeScript outputs from the new canonical location.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.

userStories.title: Make validate and build run in zero-config mode by default
userStories.description: As a modeling user, I want to run ddd-spec validate and ddd-spec build directly without understanding or writing a config file first.
acceptanceCriteria: Update packages/ddd-spec-cli/ so that when --config is not passed it reads ddd-spec/canonical/index.yaml by default.
In zero-config mode, make build write to ./.ddd-spec/artifacts/ and ./.ddd-spec/generated/.
In zero-config mode, do not require a user-supplied schema path; load the schema from package-owned resources.
When ddd-spec/canonical/index.yaml is missing, return a user-facing error that tells the user to run ddd-spec init.
If the old --config path is temporarily retained, it must remain an internal compatibility implementation detail rather than a standard user entry point.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.

userStories.title: Add minimal ddd-spec init support
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a single command to create the minimum modeling skeleton in an empty repository so that I can start authoring canonical YAML immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a ddd-spec init command to the CLI.
Make init create ddd-spec/canonical/index.yaml plus the objects/, commands/, events/, aggregates/, processes/, and vocabulary/ subdirectories.
Make init create or update .gitignore so that .ddd-spec/ is ignored.
Ensure the generated minimum index.yaml passes basic schema validation.
If ddd-spec/canonical/index.yaml already exists, init refuses to overwrite it and prints a clear message.
Init does not generate a complex example domain, industry templates, or a multi-step interactive flow.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.

userStories.title: Add zero-config ddd-spec viewer command
userStories.description: As a modeling user, I want to run ddd-spec viewer to inspect the current specification without understanding viewer artifact paths or manually composing URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a ddd-spec viewer command to the CLI.
Make the viewer command ensure that ./.ddd-spec/artifacts/viewer-spec.json is up to date before starting the viewer.
Ensure the viewer defaults to the zero-config viewer artifact and does not require the user to manually pass ?spec=....
Continue reusing the existing apps/design-spec-viewer frontend rather than introducing a new viewer stack.
Do not expand viewer functionality in this story; do not add URL upload, filtering, highlighting, or deep-link features.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.

userStories.title: Cut over public scripts and documentation to zero-config
userStories.description: As a user and repository collaborator, I want the main docs and main scripts to be organized around the zero-config path so that config-first and design-spec-first guidance no longer misleads onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: Update the primary documentation to present the standard path as init -> edit canonical -> validate -> build -> viewer.
Remove public documentation guidance that asks users to write ddd-spec.config.yaml.
Remove public documentation guidance that treats design-spec/canonical/, design-spec/artifacts/, or design-spec/generated/ as the main user path.
Switch root-level public scripts and their descriptions to the zero-config product narrative.
If legacy config-first or design-spec entry points remain for internal compatibility or quality-gate reasons, keep them out of primary documentation, primary script descriptions, and user onboarding.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.

userStories.title: Harden regression coverage for the zero-config path
userStories.description: As a repository maintainer, I want dedicated regression coverage for the zero-config user path so that future changes do not silently revert fixed conventions or break the new default flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an integration test for ddd-spec init.
Add an integration test for zero-config validate.
Add an integration test for zero-config build.
Add a minimal integration test for ddd-spec viewer.
Update or preserve the existing core, projection, and example regression tests so they remain valid under the new directory convention.
Ensure all new and existing tests plus the full quality gates continue to pass after the migration.
npm run test:ddd-spec passes
npm run verify:design-spec passes
Perform a code review of the current story changes and fix all High-severity findings before completion.
Create a commit for the current story changes.
